description: PLtetO-1 is the synthetic TetR-repressible / aTc-inducible promoter of Lutz & Bujard (1997), built by inserting two Tn10 tetO2 operators into the bacteriophage lambda PL scaffold.

references.title: Independent and tight regulation of transcriptional units in Escherichia coli via the LacR/O, the TetR/O and AraC/I1-I2 regulatory elements

references.authors: Kleinschmidt C, Tovar K, Hillen W, Porschke D
references.title: Dynamics of repressor-operator recognition: the Tn10-encoded tetracycline resistance control

references.authors: Hinrichs W, Kisker C, Duvel M, Muller A, Tovar K, Hillen W, Saenger W
references.title: Structure of the Tet repressor-tetracycline complex and regulation of antibiotic resistance

label: PLtetO-1
note: PLtetO-1 is the synthetic TetR-repressible / aTc-inducible promoter of Lutz & Bujard (1997), built by inserting two Tn10 tetO2 operators into the bacteriophage lambda PL scaffold.

label: tetO2 operator
note: Upstream Tn10 tetO2 operator (19 bp consensus TCCCTATCAGTGATAGAGA) that replaced lambda PL's upstream cI/OL site; bound by the TetR dimer.
Canonical sigma-70 -35 hexamer TTGACA from the lambda PL scaffold, flanked by the two tetO2 operators (abuts the distal operator at 19, the spacer operator a...

label: tetO2 operator (proximal)
note: Second Tn10 tetO2 operator (identical 19 bp consensus) placed in the -35/-10 spacer; TetR binding here sterically occludes RNA polymerase.
Lambda PL -10 hexamer GATACT, 17 bp downstream of the -35 (canonical spacer), overlapping the 3' end of the spacer tetO2 by 2 bp.
